steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is A, $-3k + 17r - 3$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Set up
steps.content: Substitute the expressions for $a$ and $b$: $a - b = (6k + 5r) - (9k - 12r + 3)$.

steps.title: Solve
steps.content: Distribute the negative sign: $= 6k + 5r - 9k + 12r - 3$. Combine like terms: $k$: $6k - 9k = -3k$; $r$: $5r + 12r = 17r$; constant: $-3$. Result: $-3k + 17r - 3$.
